data.title: Orna Therapeutics to present at American College of Rheumatology Meeting on in vivo therapy approach.
data.description: In a recent announcement on October 24, 2025, in Watertown, Massachusetts, Orna Therapeutics, a prominent biotechnology firm, shared plans for a forthcoming presentation at the American College of... The company is focused on developing a unique portfolio of in vivo treatments for various autoimmune and oncology conditions.

data.title: Terumo Interventions launches OPUSWAVE® Dual Sensor Imaging System & DualView® Catheter, FDA cleared. Benefits include reduced procedural time and costs, with 150mm max pullback length & 2.6 Fr. imaging profile.
data.description: The OPUSWAVE Imaging System merges OFDI and IVUS into one catheter, promising to cut down procedural time and expenses for treating coronary artery disease. The DualView imaging catheter boasts a maximum pullback length of 150mm, with a slim 2.6 Fr. imaging profile and compatibility with 6 Fr. guide catheters.

data.title: Ponce Financial Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: PDLB) reports Q3 2025 results for Ponce Bank, N.A. in New York.
data.description: On October 24, 2025, Ponce Financial Group, Inc. disclosed its third-quarter outcomes for 2025. The firm, listed as PDLB on NASDAQ, functions as the parent entity of Ponce Bank, N.A. Data regarding financial performance and statistics for this quarter were released by the company.

data.title: MAX Power has obtained drilling license for Canada's first natural hydrogen well, with drilling to start in early November by Lawson Drilling.
data.description: MAX Power is making progress on Canada's first natural hydrogen well, with Lawson Drilling set to commence operations in early November. This milestone project signifies a significant move towards sustainable energy sources, with the drilling slated to start in just a few weeks. This marks a major step in advancing Canada's energy landscape with the potential to revolutionize the industry. The development is poised to garner attention due to its innovative approach and potential to impact the renewable energy sector significantly. Stay tuned for updates as this groundbreaking project unfolds.

data.title: NeurAxis receives FDA clearance for PENFS technology treating FAP in FD patients aged 8+, a historic milestone.
data.description: NeurAxis, Inc. has received FDA clearance for its percutaneous electrical nerve field stimulation (PENFS) technology to treat functional abdominal pain (FAP) associated with functional dyspepsia (FD) in patients aged 8 years and older. The FDA approval is based on clinical trials and real-world evidence showing safety and effectiveness in pediatric patients and individuals up to 21 years of age. This milestone marks the first FDA approval for treating functional dyspepsia in adults and signifies a significant advancement in medical technology for chronic conditions.

data.title: Gentex Corp (NASDAQ: GNTX) reported Q3 2025 financial results, including revenues for the three and nine months ended Sept 30.
data.description: On October 24, 2025, in Zeeland, Michigan, Gentex Corporation (NASDAQ: GNTX) disclosed their financial performance for the periods ending September 30, 2025. Gentex is renowned for providing digital vision, connected car, dimmable glass, fire protection technologies, medical devices, and consumer electronics. The report covered the company's results for both the three-month and nine-month stretches.

data.title: OBOOK Holdings gets Money Transmitter License in Washington, expanding to 40 states for fund operations.
data.description: OBOOK Holdings Inc., trading under the symbol OWLS on the NASDAQ, also known as OwlTing Group, has successfully obtained Money Transmitter Licenses in the United States. This achievement allows them to facilitate financial transactions in Washington State. Consequently, the company can now conduct fund operations in a significant number of states in the U.S., specifically in 40 states.

data.title: CBN engages fintech leaders at IMF-World Bank meetings during 2025 annual meet, part of Nigeria's wider engagement.
data.description: On October 24, 2025, in Washington, D.C., the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) organized a fintech roundtable as part of Nigeria's participation at the 2025 IMF-World Bank Annual Meetings. The meeting aimed to discuss strategic approaches in the financial technology sector. The CBN is currently analyzing key takeaways from the session to further enhance Nigeria's engagement in the fintech industry.

data.title: Latin Metals will spin out its 100%-owned Peruvian copper projects into new company for TSXV listing in early 2026.
data.description: Latin Metals plans to separate its Peruvian copper projects, which it fully owns, into a new exploration company. This new entity aims to secure a listing on the TSXV by early 2026.

data.title: Kadena's unexpected failure stuns the crypto community as the token plummets, causing investor panic. Details revealed on the sudden collapse.
data.description: The sudden closure of Kadena has sent shockwaves throughout the cryptocurrency community. This unexpected shutdown has led to a dramatic fall in the token's value, causing panic among investors. The reasons behind this collapse are being closely examined as stakeholders try to make sense of the situation. Stay updated on this developing story for more information.

data.title: Applied Materials Inc. plans to cut global workforce by about 4% to boost competitiveness. CEO cites automation and digitalization as reasons.
data.description: Applied Materials Inc. (NASDAQ: AMAT) recently disclosed a strategy to decrease its worldwide workforce by approximately 4%, aiming to boost productivity and competitiveness for future growth. CEO Gary Dickerson explained in an email to staff that the decision was driven by changing business demands such as automation, digitalization, and shifts in geography. This move came after the company's partnership announcement with GlobalFoundries to expedite photonics development. Following the news, the stock experienced a decline in the premarket trading session on Friday. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
